acceptance_targets: [CH8-F001] PASS if the revised amounts at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:43, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:73, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:179 can be read as one continuous ledger with no unexplained large cash drop; FAIL if a reader still has to invent where missing cash went.

[CH8-F002] PASS if a cold reader can trace exactly one brake-fix origin from chapter 8 end state into chapter 9 setup without contradiction; FAIL if both a completed shop repair and a later first-time DIY front-pad change remain inferable.

[CH8-F003] PASS if the revised beat keeps the reader in concrete immediate consequence and contains no generalized moral verdict sentence in this decision moment; FAIL if the passage still resolves tension through abstract self-judgment.

[CH8-F004] PASS if at least three of the cited lines are rewritten with distinct speaker-specific syntax and lexicon and no two read as interchangeable aphorisms; FAIL if the lines still share near-identical proverb-like cadence.

[CH8-F005] PASS if no more than one doubled 'He almost...' construction remains across the cited spans and at least one replacement line shows immediate physical or situational interruption; FAIL if both moments retain the same duplicated setup.

[CH8-F006] PASS if at least two of the three cited moments are rendered as observable behavior rather than abstract self-labeling while preserving Eli's managerial-shame profile; FAIL if all three remain primarily diagnostic summary statements.

[FBR-001] Pass if a reader can trace a single, contradiction-free brake timeline from snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2399 through snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2706 without inferring two separate front-pad replacements. Fail if both a completed shop front-pad replacement and an implied off-page second front-pad replacement remain possible.

[FBR-004] Pass if neither target line uses back-to-back suppression phrasing and the pawn-shop scene still conveys emotional recoil through specific action/dialogue. Fail if the revised passage still reads as 'He almost X. He almost Y. Instead...'.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised amounts at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:43, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:73, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:179 can be read as one continuous ledger with no unexplained large cash drop; FAIL if a reader still has to invent where missing cash went.
findings.problem: Cash continuity is internally coherent inside this chapter but conflicts with the established carryover entering chapter 8, creating an unexplained disappearance of funds and weakening financial-causality trust.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile the money trail at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:43, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:73, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:179 by either adding explicit intervening expenses/transfers before the brake negotiation or revising the listed amounts so start-of-chapter cash aligns with prior state and downstream arithmetic.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a cold reader can trace exactly one brake-fix origin from chapter 8 end state into chapter 9 setup without contradiction; FAIL if both a completed shop repair and a later first-time DIY front-pad change remain inferable.
findings.problem: Brake-repair causality does not cleanly hand off to the next-chapter boundary state: this chapter depicts completed shop-performed partial repair, which conflicts with the downstream setup that attributes front-pad work to Eli doing it himself overnight.
findings.rewrite_direction: Choose one causal chain and enforce it at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:31, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:51, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:83, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:338: either keep a shop-completed repair and remove DIY implications from handoff beats, or revise this chapter so the shop does diagnosis/quote only and Eli performs the actual partial fix later that night.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised beat keeps the reader in concrete immediate consequence and contains no generalized moral verdict sentence in this decision moment; FAIL if the passage still resolves tension through abstract self-judgment.
findings.problem: Sanitization via moral safety valve: immediately after the ring sale decision, the narration shifts to abstract moral framing that softens the raw transactional discomfort instead of staying in present-tense consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:144, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:145, replace abstract moral adjudication with embodied specifics (cash urgency, legal hold reality, bodily response, or concrete next-step pressure) while preserving the decision to sell.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least three of the cited lines are rewritten with distinct speaker-specific syntax and lexicon and no two read as interchangeable aphorisms; FAIL if the lines still share near-identical proverb-like cadence.
findings.problem: Multiple speakers converge on the same polished aphoristic cadence, reducing idiolect separation and making side-character voices partially interchangeable under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework dialogue texture at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:37, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:95, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:133, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:171, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:302 so each speaker carries role-specific diction and rhythm (mechanic pragmatics, pawnbroker valuation/legal phrasing, desk-clerk local-weather register, Theo accountability register) rather than shared maxim style.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than one doubled 'He almost...' construction remains across the cited spans and at least one replacement line shows immediate physical or situational interruption; FAIL if both moments retain the same duplicated setup.
findings.problem: A repeated sentence construction (double-beat 'He almost ... He almost ...') appears at multiple emotional pivots, reading as a prose tic rather than a fresh pressure response.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary one or both repeated constructions at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:50, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:135, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:136 by converting at least one beat into concrete interruption/action/sensory trigger instead of mirrored syntactic setup.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of the three cited moments are rendered as observable behavior rather than abstract self-labeling while preserving Eli's managerial-shame profile; FAIL if all three remain primarily diagnostic summary statements.
findings.problem: Mechanical reuse of style-bible stress-tell language (managerial self-diagnosis labels) flattens interiority by naming the pattern repeatedly instead of dramatizing it.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:211, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:286, keep only one explicit meta-label and rewrite the others as enacted behavior (failed call wording, aborted action, bodily tell, or concrete procedural misstep).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can trace a single, contradiction-free brake timeline from snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2399 through snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2706 without inferring two separate front-pad replacements. Fail if both a completed shop front-pad replacement and an implied off-page second front-pad replacement remain possible.
findings.problem: Cross-chapter continuity breaks on brake-repair chronology. Chapter 8 states the shop installs front pads/flushes fluid, but Chapter 9 later claims Eli changed front pads himself the night before the crash. Because chapters are revised independently, Chapter 8 needs an unambiguous handoff state.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the mechanical-state block at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2399, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2405, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2419, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2453, and departure lines around snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2706 so this chapter establishes exactly one brake history. Option A: keep shop work as limited/temporary and explicitly set up later self-repair; Option B: keep shop-installed front pads and explicitly state no further brake work before dawn.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if neither target line uses back-to-back suppression phrasing and the pawn-shop scene still conveys emotional recoil through specific action/dialogue. Fail if the revised passage still reads as 'He almost X. He almost Y. Instead...'.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book triple-suppression pattern (4 chapters total). This chapter currently repeats the construction multiple times and should be REPLACED to avoid tic-like cadence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace suppression-template lines at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2417 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2503 with one concrete action or one spoken fragment that reveals avoidance without the repeated 'almost told/almost said' formula.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally mixed behavior, bodily consequence, and economic detail when they are causally active. In edge cases, choose the more concrete and uncomfortable rendering unless it violates factual continuity; if two renderings are equally plausible, prefer the one that increases character accountability rather than reader reassurance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene contains pain, sex, addiction, debt panic, or humiliation, abstract phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with embodied specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, legal precision, or deliberate emotional distance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter architectures across the book: confrontation chapters, document-information turns, labor-process chapters, transit interiority chapters, and quiet relational chapters. Consecutive chapters must differ in scene density or dialogue-to-interiority ratio.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary American literary realism: precise, unsentimental, image-aware, and concrete under stress.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third-person anchored to Eli, with no unmarked POV drift.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Braided cadence: reflective medium-length sentences interrupted by blunt short lines at impact moments.
